Welcome.

So glad you're joining us for lunch. Here's the quick rundown for getting up to us. Make yourself at home for the next few days. Everything you need is right here.

Register your visit → How to get in
📍 1300 S Miami AveFLOOR 35UNIT 3506
What brings you by?
The short version

A sunny lunch, 35 floors up.

You're coming up to our home on the 35th floor, skyline and bay in every window. Lunch is handled, so come hungry and relaxed. The only thing we need from you is a quick registration so reception lets you up and we can plan around any allergies.

Good company

You never quite know who's at the table.

Lunch here has a way of bringing interesting people together: founders, investors, creatives, and the occasional familiar face passing through Miami. There's no agenda and nothing to pitch, just good conversation over a good meal. Now and then those conversations quietly turn into partnerships, and a few have turned into deals worth real money. Mostly, people leave with a handful of new friends worth knowing.

You're in good hands

Settle in and stay a while.

This is our home, and we want it to feel like yours. Our house manager is also our onsite chef on Mondays and Thursdays, and looks after meals, cleaning, laundry, and anything you might need during your stay.

When you register, tell us about any food allergies or preferences and we'll have the kitchen sorted before you arrive.

Getting in

Finding your way up.

01

Park

Valet for guests is $25 (or $50 overnight) when the garage isn't full. There's also street parking, usually behind the building or the street perpendicular to it.

02

Find the entrance

We're on the corner of S Miami Ave and 13th Street, with entrances on both sides. Use the call button at the lobby to be let in.

03

Check in at the front desk

Tell reception you're visiting Unit 3506, Mike Krilivsky. They'll call up and grant elevator access.

04

Up to the 35th floor

Off the elevator, turn right and follow the hall around to #3506. Knock loudly and slip off your shoes at the door. We keep clean indoor sandals waiting for you.

Making yourself at home

Living here, by voice.

A few good-to-knows for an overnight stay, plus how to run the apartment hands-free.

Key access

Coming & going

Once you're registered, our house manager hands you a key. Heading out? Bring it and press L for the lobby. Coming back, check in at the front desk first, then press 35 to come up.

Sound

Music, anywhere

Just ask out loud in the kitchen or living room. On WiFi with an iPhone, you can also steer it from Apple's Music app.

"Hey Siri, play Rufus Du Sol"
Spare room · shades

Smart shades

Open and close them by voice only. Please don't pull them by hand, it can damage the mechanism.

"Siri, open shades" / "close shades"
Spare room · lighting

The blue lights

Set the mood with the two accent lights whenever you like.

"Siri, turn on magic" / "off magic"
The building

Pools, plunge & rooftop.

Take a tour with our house manager or Mike. Officially the amenities are for residents and their guests together, and the staff are wonderfully relaxed about it. The pool, cold plunge, and hot tub are all yours to enjoy.

  • P

    Resident & hotel amenities

    Pool, hot tub, cold plunge, steam room, sauna, gym, spa, and a bar with food and drink.

    Bar, food & towels · 11:00–17:30
  • 54

    Rooftop pool

    A private residents' pool with incredible views. Worth the trip up.

One small thing

  • Please be lovely to the building staff. They take wonderful care of us, and of you.
★ Required before arrival

Register your visit.

Two minutes. It lets reception clear you up, helps us plan around any allergies, and keeps an emergency contact on hand. A copy is emailed to you.
